response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship, TDD, and polyglot experience, but the role specifically demands a hands-on C++ developer with 8-12 years building high-performance, multi-threaded, message-broker-based industrial systems. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~6 months in 2014-2015) and no demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or industrial automation domains. Recent work is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web and platform engineering, which is a poor match for the core C++/industrial-cybersecurity profile.
strengths: 13+ years of total software engineering experience with strong craftsmanship, TDD, and architectural skills
Solid background in distributed, low-latency, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby microservices)
Cross-domain breadth including embedded, networking, web servers, and event-driven platforms
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience — last C/C++ role was ~10 years ago at National Instruments
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity exposure
Lacks industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT) and on-prem industrial platform experience

key_factors.factor: Missing IEC 62443 / SDL industrial security background
Strong general software architecture and craftsmanship
